title: Homogeneity
summary: Defines slot weights, total weight, output weight, and multihomogeneity for multilinear operators.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines slot weights, total weight, output weight, and multihomogeneity for multilinear operators.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in PolyGJMSoperators/Homogeneity.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Is Multi Homogeneous. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def IsMultiHomogeneous {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: total Weight. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def totalWeight (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: output Weight. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def outputWeight (CalConf : Calculus Conf) {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) (N : ℕ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: Is Multi Homogeneous.apply. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem IsMultiHomogeneous.apply {r : ℕ} {w : Fin r → ℝ} {F : CalConf.MultiInput r} (hF : CalConf.IsMultiHomogeneous w F) (i : Fin r) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (w i) (F i)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: total Weight fin two. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalWeight_fin_two (w : Fin 2 → ℝ) : CalConf.totalWeight w = w 0 + w 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: total Weight fin three. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem totalWeight_fin_three (w : Fin 3 → ℝ) : CalConf.totalWeight w = w 0 + w 1 + w 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: output Weight def. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_def {r : ℕ} (w : Fin r → ℝ) (N : ℕ) : CalConf.outputWeight w N = CalConf.totalWeight w - 2 * (N : ℝ)

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
